methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI pressure on Richmond's young workforce signals concentration of capability demands in metro areas, creating a sorting mechanism that deepens the metro-rural divide unless distributed training infrastructure emerges to democratize access.

signals.title: AI trainer says workers must lead technology, not follow it - WDBJ7
signals.signal_reading: An AI trainer emphasizing worker-led technology adoption in Southwest Virginia signals potential for peer-driven skill building in non-metro regions, but success depends on whether training translates to deep fluency or remains motivational messaging.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's institutional AI partnership concentrates expertise and resources in Richmond Metro, creating a hub-and-spoke model that could either radiate capability outward or reinforce the capital region as an exclusive center of AI innovation.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Hampton Roads MLS deploying an AI tool for home searches represents platform-delivered capability creating broad but shallow adoption—real estate agents gain AI functionality without developing underlying technical fluency.

signals.title: AI is here in Hampton Roads, and businesses and organizations are embracing it - MSN
signals.signal_reading: Hampton Roads businesses embracing AI signals distributed adoption through turnkey platforms rather than indigenous development, creating geographic breadth but potentially shallow implementation that leaves regions dependent on external vendors.
